subject: [PATCH v2 1/2] mm/vmstat, mm/memcontrol: add _monotonic vmstat readers

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch adds new functions to the Linux kernel that provide monotonic, non-clamping readers for certain vmstat counters. These counters are used by some applications to track monotonically-incremented event counts and compute deltas between two samples. Without these new functions, a signed-long wraparound could cause incorrect results when computing these deltas. The patch adds three new functions: global_node_page_state_monotonic(), node_page_state_monotonic(), and lruvec_page_state_monotonic(). These functions are only safe for use with monotonically-incremented counters and do not clamp negative reads to zero, unlike the existing clamped helpers. This patch is a prerequisite for further changes that will replace producer-side accumulators with read-side accumulators in prepare_scan_control().
